PAPERLESS_PORT.description: Host loopback port the paperless webserver (Web-UI + REST API) binds on. NPM proxies the public `paperless.<publicDomain>` down to this port behind Authelia forward-auth; the #931 PaperlessIngest adapter reaches the REST API on it via host loopback (127.0.0.1:<port>). Default 8000. NB: the servicebay.healthcheck annotation uses the literal 8000 — change it there too if you change this.

PAPERLESS_REDIS_PORT.description: Loopback port the bundled redis task broker binds on. Only reachable inside the shared host netns; change only if it collides with another loopback service.

PAPERLESS_DB_PORT.description: Loopback port the bundled postgres binds on. Only reachable inside the shared host netns; the default is offset from the stock 5432 so it doesn't collide with any other postgres on the box.

PAPERLESS_DB_PASSWORD.description: Password for the bundled postgres `paperless` user, shared between the postgres and webserver containers. Postgres binds loopback-only inside the shared host netns, so this is an intra-pod namespace boundary rather than an internet-facing secret — but change the default to your own value on a shared box.

PAPERLESS_OCR_LANGUAGE.description: OCR / date-parsing language hint (Tesseract lang codes, `+`-joined). Default `deu+eng`. NOTE: paperless's own OCR is DISABLED (PAPERLESS_OCR_MODE=skip in template.yml) because its Tesseract regresses rotated German scans to garble (PoC #929) — Solaris's gemma4:12b vision extractor stays the text source and the #931 adapter PATCHes that clean text into paperless. This value only affects date parsing + the UI locale, not OCR.

PAPERLESS_UID.description: Host uid paperless runs its worker + owns the consume/media/data files as. Default 1000 matches the file-share Syncthing share owner so scans dropped into the shared consume dir are readable.

PAPERLESS_GID.description: Host gid paperless owns its files as. Default 1000, matching the file-share share group.

PAPERLESS_SUBDOMAIN.description: Subdomain for the paperless Web-UI + REST API. Internal exposure — gets a cert and a LAN-only access list, plus Authelia forward-auth on top: paperless trusts the forwarded Remote-User header (PAPERLESS_ENABLE_HTTP_REMOTE_USER) so there's no second login. The #931 PaperlessIngest adapter does NOT go through this route — it reaches the API on the host loopback (127.0.0.1:PAPERLESS_PORT), bypassing forward-auth with a paperless API token.
